prerequisites: A NinjaOne account with system administrator access. Per NinjaOne's documentation, only system administrators may configure OAuth tokens for the NinjaOne API.
An API client application created in NinjaOne under Administration > Apps > API, which issues the credential supplied in the connector's required api_key field.
A start_date value (the connector's required start_date config field) marking the earliest point from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: API client credential (OAuth 2.0)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with the single required secret field api_key. You register an API client application in NinjaOne (Administration > Apps > API, Client App IDs tab), which issues a Client ID and a Client Secret. NinjaOne's OAuth token configuration lists Client Credentials as an available grant type for machine-to-machine access, which exchanges these credentials for a short-lived bearer access token. Grant the application the Monitoring scope, which grants read-only access to monitoring data and organization structure, sufficient for a read-only source connector. Supply the issued credential as the connector's api_key.

setupSteps.title: Sign in as a system administrator
setupSteps.body: Log in to NinjaOne with an account that has system administrator privileges. NinjaOne restricts OAuth token configuration to system administrators.

setupSteps.title: Create an API client application
setupSteps.body: Go to Administration > Apps > API, open the Client App IDs tab, and select Add to open the application configuration and create a new API client application.

setupSteps.title: Select the scope and grant type
setupSteps.body: Assign the Monitoring scope, which grants read-only access to monitoring data and organization structure (Management is for modifying device and organization information and Control is for remote access, neither of which a read-only source requires). Choose the grant type that matches how the connector obtains a token; for unattended machine-to-machine syncing this is the Client Credentials grant.

setupSteps.title: Save and capture the credential
setupSteps.body: Save the application. NinjaOne issues a Client ID and a Client Secret. Copy and store these immediately.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the captured API client credential into the connector's required api_key field, and set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which data should be synced.

sources.title: How to Set Up API OAuth Token - NinjaOne
